**Critical Left** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Sinistra Critica*, **SC**) was a [communist](/wiki/Communism "Communism") and [Trotskyist](/wiki/Trotskyism "Trotskyism") [political party in Italy](/wiki/List_of_political_parties_in_Italy "List of political parties in Italy"), affiliated to the [Fourth International](/wiki/Fourth_International_(post-reunification) "Fourth International (post-reunification)").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Critical_Left&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Originally a [Trotskyist](/wiki/Trotskyism "Trotskyism") faction within the [Communist Refoundation Party](/wiki/Communist_Refoundation_Party "Communist Refoundation Party") (PRC), it broke ranks on 8 December 2007 to become an independent political party.[[1]](#cite_note-1) From 2006 to 2008, SC had [deputy](/wiki/Italian_Chamber_of_Deputies "Italian Chamber of Deputies") [Salvatore Cannavò](/w/index.php?title=Salvatore_Cannav%C3%B2&action=edit&redlink=1 "Salvatore Cannavò (page does not exist)") and [senator](/wiki/Senate_of_the_Republic_(Italy) "Senate of the Republic (Italy)") [Franco Turigliatto](/wiki/Franco_Turigliatto "Franco Turigliatto") who voted consistently against the [Prodi II Cabinet](/wiki/Prodi_II_Cabinet "Prodi II Cabinet") and the latter was responsible of the first major crisis of the government on 22 February 2007.[[2]](#cite_note-2) After this, Turigliatto was expelled from the PRC in March.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In the [2008 general election](/wiki/2008_Italian_general_election "2008 Italian general election"), SC (890 members) ran its own lists and [Flavia D'Angeli](/w/index.php?title=Flavia_D%27Angeli&action=edit&redlink=1 "Flavia D'Angeli (page does not exist)") was chosen as candidate for Prime Minister of the party. In the election, SC gained 0.5% of the national vote.

SC dissolved in July 2013 and agreed to separate into two groupings which would each try out their different approaches, with the first grouping formed a new party called [Anticapitalist Left](/wiki/Anticapitalist_Left_(Italy) "Anticapitalist Left (Italy)") while the second founded Internationalist Solidarity, a political association uninvolved in direct elections.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Leadership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Critical_Left&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Leadership")]

* Spokespersons: [Salvatore Cannavò](/w/index.php?title=Salvatore_Cannav%C3%B2&action=edit&redlink=1 "Salvatore Cannavò (page does not exist)") (2007–2009), [Flavia D'Angeli](/w/index.php?title=Flavia_D%27Angeli&action=edit&redlink=1 "Flavia D'Angeli (page does not exist)") (2008–present), [Franco Turigliatto](/wiki/Franco_Turigliatto "Franco Turigliatto") (2009–present) and [Piero Maestri](/w/index.php?title=Piero_Maestri&action=edit&redlink=1 "Piero Maestri (page does not exist)") (2009–2013)
